ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general black tea beverage class; no specific entry for Teh O Ice (iced unsweetened black tea, Singapore style). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers black tea as a beverage category. No specific code for iced unsweetened black tea (Teh O Ice). Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 refers to black tea as a substance. No specific code for the iced beverage preparation. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Teh O Ice is approximately 250 ml (250 g), served in a glass or cup over ice, with no added sugar or milk.

nutrient_source.title: Black tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Teh O Ice is unsweetened black tea served over ice. The USDA FoodData Central entry for brewed black tea (no sugar, no milk) is an exact compositional match. Ice dilution is negligible for per-100g values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages: plain brewed black tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as effectively zero
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain unsweetened black tea contains negligible carbohydrates (0.3 g per 100 g, of which glycemic carbohydrates are effectively zero). The University of Sydney GI Database does not list a specific GI for plain black tea because it contains no glycemic carbohydrates. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) per Project Veritas convention for zero-glycemic beverages. GL per 250 g serving = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (3 mg per 100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Teh O Ice is an unsweetened black tea with very low sodium (approximately 7.5 mg per 250 ml serving), making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension, provided no sugar or condensed milk is added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; no added sugar in the plain preparation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain Teh O Ice contains effectively zero glycemic carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of 0 per serving, making it a safe beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, patients should confirm no sugar is added at point of preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols with potential cardioprotective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh O Ice contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no added sugar in its plain form; black tea polyphenols (flavonoids) have been associated with modest improvements in endothelial function, supporting its inclusion in a heart-healthy diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh O Ice is a popular Singaporean iced beverage consisting of plain black tea brewed from Ceylon or local tea leaves, served over ice without milk or sugar. The name derives from Hokkien: 'Teh' means tea, 'O' means black (without milk), and 'Ice' or 'Ais' indicates it is served cold. It is a staple at kopitiams (traditional Singaporean coffee shops), hawker centres, and food courts across Singapore and Malaysia. The drink is characterised by its deep amber colour, slightly astringent and mildly bitter flavour, and refreshing quality when served cold. Nutritionally, plain Teh O Ice is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 g), contains no fat, no protein, negligible carbohydrates (0.3 g per 100 g), and trace minerals including potassium (37 mg per 100 g) and magnesium (3 mg per 100 g). It contains naturally occurring tea polyphenols and caffeine. Because it contains no added sugar, no milk, and no glycemic carbohydrates, it is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. It should be distinguished from Teh Ice (black tea with condensed milk and ice) and Teh O Ice Limau (iced black tea with lime juice). The unsweetened plain version is the healthiest preparation and is widely available at SGD 1.00–1.50 at hawker centres, though premium café versions may cost more.
common_user_queries: Does Teh O Ice have sugar?
How many calories in Teh O Ice?

meal_context: accompaniment to roti prata or nasi lemak

confidence_reason: Teh O Ice is plain unsweetened brewed black tea served over ice. The USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea (NDB 14355) is an exact compositional match. Nutrient values are well-established and consistent across multiple approved sources. GI is effectively zero due to absence of glycemic carbohydrates, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database conventions for non-glycemic beverages.
notes: Teh O Ice is plain unsweetened black tea served over ice. protein_g is naturally zero as brewed black tea contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as brewed black tea contains no fat. sugar_g is confirmed zero for the plain unsweetened preparation (no added sugar). dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed black tea. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g (trace energy from negligible carbohydrates), confirmed per USDA FoodData Central. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for zero-glycemic beverages. GL per 250 g serving = 0.0. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 as brewed black tea contains no fat fractions; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0 as reported at or below detection threshold in USDA FoodData Central for brewed black tea; confirmed zero or trace. calcium_mg is 0.0 per USDA FoodData Central for brewed black tea. This record covers the plain unsweetened preparation only. Sweetened variants (Teh O Ice with sugar, Teh O Ice Limau) require separate records. The cuisine tag in the input was listed as 'Indian' but Teh O Ice is a pan-Singaporean/Malaysian beverage served across all cuisine contexts; cuisine has been corrected to 'Singaporean' as this is the canonical cultural origin for this preparation style.
